channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Stalemate with Iran puts Trump's second term to the test
item.description: President Trump's stalemate in Iran spells trouble for the rest of his second term.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's stalemate in Iran spells trouble for the rest of his second term.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez

item.title: Republican strategist on how the Iran war is affecting Trump politically
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Republican strategist Rina Shah about the political ramifications of the war in Iran for President Trump.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Republican strategist Rina Shah about the political ramifications of the war in Iran for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: 'There's no crime here': Legal experts weigh in on DOJ's indictment of James Comey
item.description: Legal experts say the indictment against former FBI Director James Comey lacks evidence of a true criminal threat.
item.content:encoded: Legal experts say the indictment against former FBI Director James Comey lacks evidence of a true criminal threat.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: Former Surgeon General Jerome Adams on Trump's new pick for the role
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams about President Trump's latest nominee for the role, Dr. Nicole Saphier.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former Surgeon General Dr. Jerome Adams about President Trump's latest nominee for the role, Dr. Nicole Saphier.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Passenger railroads see a sharp jump in ridership as gas prices climb
item.description: More Americans are turning to the train as gas prices reach their highest point since the war in Iran began. Brightline, the privately run railroad in Florida, had its best month ever in March.
item.content:encoded:
More Americans are turning to the train as gas prices reach their highest point since the war in Iran began. Brightline, the privately run railroad in Florida, had its best month ever in March.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Regional efforts to save native seeds aims to combat effects of climate change
item.description: As climate change, wildfires and other extreme weather events intensify, the demand for native seeds is surging in order to help preserve plant biodiversity.
item.content:encoded: As climate change, wildfires and other extreme weather events intensify, the demand for native seeds is surging in order to help preserve plant biodiversity.
item.dc:creator: Juanpablo Ramirez-Franco

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Negotiations between the U.S. and Iran at a stalemate, Congress votes to end record DHS shutdown, Trump announces new nominee for surgeon general.
item.content:encoded: Negotiations between the U.S. and Iran at a stalemate, Congress votes to end record DHS shutdown, Trump announces new nominee for surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: The effects of the Iran war on Americans' wallet
item.description: Gas prices just hit a 4-year record, and the price of jet fuel has doubled. NPR speaks to industry executives and energy experts to explain the areas in which Americans are feeling the pinch.
item.content:encoded: Gas prices just hit a 4-year record, and the price of jet fuel has doubled. NPR speaks to industry executives and energy experts to explain the areas in which Americans are feeling the pinch.
item.dc:creator: Natalie Weber

item.title: The Iran war has caused oil and gas prices to skyrocket. How it's affecting consumers
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks business news analyst Jill Schlesinger how high oil and gas prices from the Iran war affect consumer budgets.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep asks business news analyst Jill Schlesinger how high oil and gas prices from the Iran war affect consumer budgets.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Camp Mystic, scene of Texas flooding disaster, won't reopen this summer
item.description: Camp Mystic, the private all-girls camp where 27 people died in last year's flooding in central Texas, announced that it is withdrawing its application to reopen this summer.
item.content:encoded: Camp Mystic, the private all-girls camp where 27 people died in last year's flooding in central Texas, announced that it is withdrawing its application to reopen this summer.
item.dc:creator: Kailey Hunt
